objective: Maximize persistence skill of a 72h z500/t850 WeatherBench-2 forecast model built under a fixed compute budget
metric: persistence skill score (1 - mean(rmse/persistence); >0 beats persistence)

criteria.outcome: model.py exists defining train_and_predict(Xtr,Ytr,Xte,meta,time_budget_s,device) that predicts the residual (Y-X), normalizes inputs with meta['mean']/['std'], and uses circular lon padding
criteria.observe: Read model.py and confirm the signature, residual target, normalization, and circular padding on lon axis are present

criteria.outcome: A submitted forecast beats persistence: persistence skill score > 0
criteria.observe: Read the submission/evaluation output and confirm reported skill = 1 - mean(rmse/persistence) is strictly positive

criteria.outcome: Predictions are finite and training respects the fixed compute budget without overrunning time_budget_s
criteria.observe: Check evaluation logs show no NaN/Inf and wall time <= time_budget_s (no overrun penalty triggered)

criteria.outcome: At least one iteration of improvement was made and the best-scoring model is kept
criteria.observe: Read run history showing >=2 submits and that the reported best skill corresponds to the retained model
